title: Cluster-configurational study of G-center in Silicon
abstract: Understanding the properties of defects is imperative for proper use for variety of applications including quantum computing. In this paper, we use the multiconfigurational self consistent field (MCSCF) combined with DFT optimized geometry in order to investigate the spin and optical properties of G centers in Silicon. By utilizing quantum chemistry based methods, we show excellent agreement with the Zero Phonon Line and Zero Field Splitting Tensor components of the G center. We also calculate the theoretical spin decoherence time of the G centers using Cluster Correlation Expansion (CCE) methods.

bite.hook: Quantum chemistry methods achieve excellent agreement with experimental spin and optical properties of G-centers in Silicon.
tldr: They investigated the spin and optical properties of G-center defects in silicon.
They combined multiconfigurational self-consistent field (MCSCF) methods with DFT-optimized geometries.
They successfully calculated the Zero Phonon Line, Zero Field Splitting Tensor, and theoretical spin decoherence times.
bite.whyItMatters: G-centers in silicon are promising candidates for quantum communication and quantum computing; understanding their precise physical properties helps engineers design better quantum hardware.
bite.eli5: Sometimes, a tiny imperfection or 'defect' in a silicon crystal can act like a perfect cage to hold a single quantum particle. Scientists used advanced computer simulations to map out exactly how these cages interact with light and magnetic fields, helping us use them to store quantum data.

glossary.shortDef: A specific carbon-related radiation damage defect in silicon.
glossary.eli5Def: A tiny microscopic defect in silicon that can trap light and spin, making it useful for quantum technology.

glossary.shortDef: The purely electronic transition in the optical spectrum of a defect.
glossary.eli5Def: The sharpest, cleanest color of light emitted by a defect when it doesn't lose any energy to heat or vibrations.

glossary.shortDef: The loss of quantum coherence in a spin system due to environmental interactions.
glossary.eli5Def: How long a quantum particle can hold its secret information before the surrounding environment messes it up.
